paper_title: An investigation into English preparatory programs for EMI learning in higher education institutes in Vietnam

entities.description: The use of the English language to teach academic subjects in countries where the first language of the majority is not English.
The level of English language competence required for students to follow EMI programs.
A Vietnamese national initiative to improve foreign language teaching and learning.
The context driving the need for English competence and internationalization in higher education.
Research design used to examine expectations and preparations for EMI.
Systematic procedure for reviewing or evaluating documents to collect data.
Non-probability sampling used to select eight specific universities for the study.
Programs designed to bridge the English proficiency gap for students entering EMI courses.
Data sources collected from eight Vietnamese universities for content analysis.
English requirement for graduation at Banking University of Ho Chi Minh City.
English requirement for graduation at Hanoi Foreign Trade University.
English requirement for graduation at University of Da Nang - University of Science and Technology.
Minimum English proficiency level required for EMI graduation at Can Tho University.
The maximum number of credits for English preparatory programs (Hanoi National Economics University).
Percentage of newly enrolled students at Da Nang University lacking English skills for EMI.
The primary field of research concerning language policy and EMI implementation.
Researcher from the Department of General English and English for Specific Purposes.
Researcher from the Department of English Language and Culture.
Higher education institution located in Vietnam.
